items.title: Komst Chermont naar Ajax voorlopig van de baan na inmenging Cruijff
items.summary: De komst van JP Chermont naar Ajax is voorlopig op de lange baan geschoven. De club had zich bij Santos gemeld voor de talentvolle Braziliaanse rechtsback, maar technisch directeur Jordi Cruijff vindt zijn komst nu geen prioriteit hebben.

items.title: Ajax gaat voor Zinchenko
items.summary: Ajax heeft zich bij Arsenal gemeld om Oleksandr Zinchenko te huren. De gesprekken met die club over de 29-jarige linksback bevinden zich in de afrondende fase.

items.title: Wolves v Newcastle United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 2pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail John 1 min: Sandro Tonali takes the ball deep as Newcastle attempt to pass their way through. Harvey Barnes gets an early touch from a Woltemade layoff. Good energy from the home fans. How long will that last? Here’s Jeff Beck’s Hi Ho Silver Lining, with the Led Zep medley you will always hear in pre-match at Molineux. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: FC Utrecht jaagt op gelijkmaker, Twente verder met tien man
items.summary: Zondag is het Derby Day in de Eredivisie. We trappen af met de Derby van het Noorden tussen SC Heerenveen en FC Groningen, vervolgen met de Twentse Derby Heracles Almelo - FC Twente en sluiten af met het treffen tussen Feyenoord en Sparta Rotterdam, dat in een apart liveblog wordt behandeld. Verder is er ook buitenlands voetbal. Volg het hier in de VI Live.

items.title: FC Utrecht ziet geëmotioneerde Jensen opnieuw geblesseerd raken
items.summary: FC Utrecht heeft zondagmiddag een flinke domper te verwerken gekregen. De net van een blessure herstelde Victor Jensen moest andermaal met een kwetsuur van het veld. Hij werd in het duel met FC Volendam onder begeleiding naar de catacomben gebracht.

items.title: Utd's Martinez hits back over 'toddler' criticism
items.summary: Lisandro Martínez has challenged Paul Scholes and Nicky Butt to repeat their criticism to his face after the pair said Erling Haaland would make the Manchester United defender look like "a little toddler" in the Manchester derby.

items.title: VI Live: één wijziging bij Sparta voor duel met Feyenoord
items.summary: Zondagmiddag wordt het Eredivisie-weekend afgesloten door Feyenoord en Sparta Rotterdam. De laatste twee keer dat deze derby werd gespeeld, ging Feyenoord er met een ruime zege (0-4 en 3-0) vandoor. Volg deze wedstrijd in de VI Live.

items.title: Police investigating elbow incident in grassroots match
items.summary: Police are investigating an incident in which a football player appeared to deliberately elbow an opponent in the face during a Welsh grassroots match.

items.title: Huge missed chance or a valuable point for Arsenal? Only time will tell
items.summary: Arsenal failed to claim all three points vs. Forest, but their cautious approach might still pay off come May.

items.title: Sources: PSG finalizing deal for Barcelona starlet
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain are finalising the remaining details for the transfer of Dro Fernández, the latest prodigy from Barcelona's academy, sources confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal denied clear penalty in Forest draw
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has said Arsenal were denied a "very clear penalty" in Saturday's 0-0 draw at Nottingham Forest.

items.title: Margin for error quickly vanishing for this uninspiring Liverpool side
items.summary: A draw vs. Burnley is the latest disappointment for Liverpool as their dramatic fall from last season continues.

items.title: 'No-one says anything in your face' - Martinez hits back at Scholes and Butt
items.summary: Pundits discussing Man Utd "can talk on the television" but "no-one says anything" to your face, according to defender Lisandro Martinez.

items.title: Spurs board hold talks over Frank future
items.summary: Tottenham head coach Thomas Frank is under increasing pressure to keep his job.

items.title: Raducanu revels in new nickname and eager to 'improve a lot more'
items.summary: Emma Raducanu was thrilled with her straight-sets win in the opening round of the Australian Open on Sunday, but insists she can "improve a lot more".

items.title: Will it click for Gauff? 'When it comes together, she will be frightening!'
items.summary: Will it click for Coco Gauff? Jonathan Overend believes the American world No 3 can have a great Australian Open and lift the Daphne Akhurst Memorial Cup in Melbourne.

items.title: Elvira snatches Dubai title after Lowry's late blunder and McIlroy's charge
items.summary: Nacho Elvira capitalised on a final-hole blunder from Shane Lowry and Sunday charge from Rory McIlroy to claim a dramatic victory at the DP World Tour's Dubai Invitational.

items.title: Williams blows golden chance for Grand Slam win at 45
items.summary: Venus Williams came agonisingly close to claiming a first Grand Slam win since 2021, at the age of 45, only to lose out to Olga Danilovic as she rallied from 4-0 down in the final set to clinch a memorable victory.

items.title: Crystal Palace decide not to sack manager Oliver Glasner after meeting
items.summary: Steve Parish held talks with Austrian on Sunday morning Manager said board had ‘abandoned’ him on Saturday Crystal Palace have decided against sacking Oliver Glasner despite him accusing the club’s board of abandoning him and the rest of his squad by selling captain Marc Guéhi to Manchester City 24 hours before they were due to face Sunderland. Glasner’s outburst after the 2-1 defeat, when the Austrian also admitted he doesn’t care if he sees out his contract until the end of the season having already confirmed he will leave Selhurst Park, is understood to have dismayed Palace’s chair, Steve Parish. He was said to be weighing up if there was any other option than parting company with the manager who led Palace to their first major trophy when they won the FA Cup only eight months ago after Glasner went public over his dissatisfaction in two extraordinary press conferences. Continue reading...

items.title: Lisandro Martínez hits back at Scholes and Manchester United punditocracy
items.summary: Former players Scholes and Butt critical before derby ‘If he wants to say something … he can come to my house’ Lisandro Martínez has criticised Paul Scholes for mocking him on a podcast but not directly to the Manchester United defender’s face, following Saturday’s 2-0 win over Manchester City at Old Trafford. Scholes and Nicky Butt, another prominent former United player, were each scathing about the diminutive Martínez and his ability to mark Erling Haaland, when speaking on the Good, the Bad and the Ugly before the 198th derby. Continue reading...
